articles.title: Afrika Bambaataa, Hip-Hop Pioneer, Dies at 67
articles.summary: Afrika Bambaataa, the DJ widely credited as one of the founding fathers of hip-hop and the creator of the Universal Zulu Nation, died at age 67. His death marks the end of an era for a genre he helped build, though his final decade was defined by serious accusations of sexual abuse.
articles.content:
Afrika Bambaataa was one of the people who made hip-hop real. In the South Bronx in the 1970s and early 80s, he helped turn block parties into a global movement. His 1982 track "Planet Rock" fused electronic music with hip-hop in a way nobody had heard before, and the Universal Zulu Nation, the cultural organisation he founded, spread hip-hop values across the world.

But his legacy is complicated and it has been for a while. Starting around 2016, multiple men came forward publicly accusing Afrika Bambaataa of sexually abusing them when they were teenagers. He denied the allegations, but the accusations cast a long shadow over everything that came before. The Universal Zulu Nation, which he led for decades, was shaken by the fallout.

His death at 67 will spark real conversations about how we hold two truths at once. The man shaped a culture that billions of people love. He also faced serious, credible accusations that never went away. Both things are part of the story now.
keyPoints: Afrika Bambaataa died at age 67
He was a foundational figure in hip-hop history and helped create the genre's early identity in the South Bronx
His 1982 track Planet Rock is considered a landmark in music history
He founded the Universal Zulu Nation, a global hip-hop cultural organisation
In his final decade multiple men accused him of sexually abusing them as teenagers

articles.title: Lawsuit Claims ChatGPT Fueled Stalker's Dangerous Delusions
articles.summary: A stalking victim has sued OpenAI, alleging that ChatGPT amplified her abuser's delusions and that OpenAI ignored three separate warnings about his dangerous behaviour, including one of its own internal mass-casualty flags. The case raises urgent questions about whether AI companies are doing enough when users show signs of real-world violence.
articles.content:
A woman is suing OpenAI after she says ChatGPT made her stalking nightmare so much worse. According to the lawsuit, her ex-boyfriend used ChatGPT in ways that fed his delusions about their relationship. She claims she warned OpenAI three times that this user was dangerous. OpenAI apparently did nothing. One of those warnings even came from OpenAI's own internal system, which had flagged the user as a potential mass-casualty risk.

This is a serious legal and ethical flashpoint for OpenAI. ChatGPT is the world's most widely used AI chatbot, with hundreds of millions of users. The big question the lawsuit raises is simple: if an AI company's own safety systems flag someone as dangerous, what responsibility do they have to act? Ignoring three warnings, including one from your own tool, is going to be very hard to defend in court.

OpenAI has not publicly responded to the specific allegations yet. But this lawsuit lands at the worst possible time for the company, coming on the same day as the attack on Sam Altman's home. The pressure on OpenAI to take safety seriously, both online and off, just got a lot louder.
keyPoints: A stalking victim is suing OpenAI over ChatGPT's role in her abuse
OpenAI allegedly ignored three warnings that the user was dangerous
One of those warnings came from OpenAI's own mass-casualty flag system
The case could set a major legal precedent for AI platform responsibility
OpenAI has not yet publicly responded to the allegations

articles.title: Anitta and Shakira Drop Banger 'Choka Choka' Together
articles.summary: Anitta and Shakira have joined forces on a brand new track called 'Choka Choka', the latest single from Anitta's upcoming album EQUILIBRIVM. Two of Latin music's biggest names on one song is exactly the kind of collab the internet is going to lose its mind over.
articles.content:
Anitta and Shakira just gave us the Friday gift we did not know we needed. The two artists have teamed up on a track called 'Choka Choka', and it is already turning heads. The song is part of Anitta's new album EQUILIBRIVM, which is shaping up to be one of the most talked about Latin records of 2026.

Shakira has been on an absolute tear lately. After her 2023 breakup anthem 'BZRP Music Sessions Vol. 53' made her a global trending topic all over again, she has been incredibly selective about features. Landing on an Anitta album is a big deal. And Anitta has been building toward this moment, blending Brazilian funk with global pop ambition for years now.

'Choka Choka' sounds like a summer anthem in waiting. If EQUILIBRIVM keeps delivering at this level, Anitta is going to have a serious contender for Latin album of the year on her hands. Stream it now and get ahead of the curve before everyone else catches up.
keyPoints: Anitta and Shakira have released a new collab called 'Choka Choka'
The track is part of Anitta's new album EQUILIBRIVM
Shakira has been highly selective with features since her 2023 comeback
Both artists are among the biggest names in global Latin music right now

articles.title: AI Fake Relationship Gurus Are Racking Up Millions of Views
articles.summary: Wired reports that AI-generated fake relationship podcast hosts are going viral with videos pushing old-fashioned gender tropes, while quietly funnelling viewers toward AI influencer schools. The trend matters because it shows how easily AI content can spread harmful ideas at massive scale while making money off the people watching.
articles.content:
Imagine a podcast host who looks confident, sounds authoritative, and tells you exactly how to keep a man happy. Now imagine that host is entirely fake, built by AI, and designed not to help you but to sell you a course. That is exactly what Wired has uncovered, and the videos are pulling in millions of views across social platforms.

These AI-generated relationship gurus are recycling gender stereotypes that feel lifted straight from the 1950s. The content is built to feel personal and relatable, which makes it hit harder than a banner ad ever could. And at the end of the rabbit hole, there is always a link to an AI influencer school, a paid programme teaching people to build their own AI content personalities.

This is a genuinely new kind of influence operation. It is not a foreign government or a political group. It is entrepreneurs using AI to manufacture fake authority figures, harvest attention, and turn that attention into cash. The scale is the scary part. When millions of people are watching fake experts talk about relationships, that shapes real beliefs in the real world.
keyPoints: AI-generated fake podcast hosts are going viral with relationship advice content
The videos push traditional gender tropes and are designed to look authentic
The content funnels viewers toward paid AI influencer schools
This is a new kind of monetised misinformation that targets everyday emotions
The trend shows AI can now manufacture fake authority at industrial scale
articles.wikiSearchQuery: AI generated influencers social media misinformation

articles.title: Megan Thee Stallion Lights Up Broadway in Moulin Rouge!
articles.summary: Megan Thee Stallion made her Broadway debut in Moulin Rouge! and by all accounts she is turning every performance into an event. Rolling Stone reports that the Houston rap star is a standout in the role, transforming the Al Hirschfeld Theatre into what they are calling a nightly Hot Girl summit.
articles.content:
Moulin Rouge! The Musical opened on Broadway in 2018 and has been running strong ever since. The show is based on Baz Luhrmann's 2001 film and is packed with pop songs rewritten to fit a story set in 1890s Paris. It won ten Tony Awards including Best Musical in 2021. Tonys are the top prize in American theatre, think of them as the Oscars for Broadway. Now Megan Thee Stallion has stepped into it and the energy in the room has completely shifted.

Rolling Stone was on the scene and the verdict is glowing. Megan Thee Stallion is described as a sparkling diamond in the production. Her fan base, the Hot Girl community, has turned up in full force. The theatre has the kind of electricity you usually only get at a concert. That is a remarkable thing to pull off on a Broadway stage eight shows a week.

This is Megan Thee Stallion's Broadway debut and it feels like a genuine cultural crossover moment. She has had a turbulent few years, navigating a high-profile legal dispute with her former label 1501 Certified Entertainment and the fallout from the Tory Lanez trial. Stepping into a beloved Broadway show and landing this well? It is a proper reset and a reminder of just how magnetic she is as a performer.
keyPoints: Megan Thee Stallion has made her Broadway debut in Moulin Rouge! The Musical
Rolling Stone called her a sparkling diamond in the production
Her presence has turned the Al Hirschfeld Theatre into what Rolling Stone describes as a nightly Hot Girl summit
Moulin Rouge! The Musical won ten Tony Awards including Best Musical in 2021
This is a major cultural crossover moment for Megan Thee Stallion after a turbulent few years

articles.title: Labrinth Is Out as Euphoria Season 3 Composer
articles.summary: Labrinth, the British musician who became synonymous with Euphoria's haunting sound, has confirmed he is no longer involved with the soundtrack for Season 3. He made the announcement in a since-deleted expletive-filled Instagram post where he called out both his label and the show.
articles.content:
Labrinth did not go quietly. The London-born producer and singer, whose real name is Timothy Lee McKenzie, posted a furious message on Instagram calling out his record label and Euphoria in no uncertain terms before deleting it. The post is gone but the news is not. Labrinth is officially off the Season 3 soundtrack.

This is a genuinely big deal for the show. Labrinth's music was not just background noise on Euphoria. It was part of the show's DNA. His score for Seasons 1 and 2 on HBO helped define the entire emotional tone of the series, a raw, gospel-tinged sound that matched the chaos and heartbreak on screen. The Emmy-winning HBO drama follows a group of teenagers dealing with addiction, trauma, and identity, and Labrinth's music made those themes hit harder.

Season 3 of Euphoria is already one of the most talked-about upcoming TV productions, partly because it has been delayed for years and partly because the show lost its star Angus Cloud to a tragic death in 2023. Losing Labrinth too is another blow to continuity. Whoever HBO brings in to replace him has enormous shoes to fill.
keyPoints: Labrinth has confirmed he will not be scoring Euphoria Season 3
He announced the split in an angry Instagram post that was later deleted
Labrinth's music was central to Euphoria's identity across its first two seasons
Euphoria Season 3 is already under pressure after years of delays and the death of cast member Angus Cloud
It is unclear who will handle the Season 3 soundtrack in Labrinth's place

articles.title: Meta's Muse Spark AI Model Rockets Up the App Store
articles.summary: Meta Superintelligence Labs released its first AI model, Muse Spark, and Apple App Store rankings show it shot straight to the top of the charts. Meta just made a very loud entrance into the AI model race.
articles.content:
Meta Superintelligence Labs dropped Muse Spark, its debut AI model, and the response has been huge. Apple App Store data shows Meta's AI app climbing rapidly up the rankings right after the release. That kind of chart movement is a clear sign that a lot of people downloaded and tried Muse Spark very quickly.

Meta has been building toward this moment for a while. Meta Superintelligence Labs is the division set up specifically to compete with the likes of OpenAI and Google DeepMind in the AI model space. Muse Spark is the first public product to come out of that lab, so the stakes for this launch were high.

The App Store rankings do not lie. When a new AI model gets this kind of immediate traction, it means the pitch is landing with everyday users, not just tech enthusiasts. Meta clearly knows how to get something in front of a massive audience, and Muse Spark is already proving that Meta Superintelligence Labs is a real player worth watching.
keyPoints: Meta Superintelligence Labs launched its first AI model called Muse Spark
Muse Spark sent Meta's AI app surging up the Apple App Store rankings
Meta Superintelligence Labs was created to compete with OpenAI and Google DeepMind
Muse Spark is the lab's first public-facing product
The fast chart rise shows strong early adoption from everyday users
articles.wikiSearchQuery: Meta Superintelligence Labs Muse Spark AI model

articles.title: Bruno Mars Launches First World Tour in Nearly a Decade
articles.summary: Bruno Mars kicks off The Romantic Tour tonight, his first world tour in almost ten years and his first ever shows at American football stadiums. After years of residency shows in Las Vegas, Bruno Mars is back on the road in a massive way.
articles.content:
Bruno Mars has been living in Las Vegas for the past few years, packing out the MGM Grand Garden Arena almost every weekend. It made him one of the highest-earning live acts on the planet without ever hitting the road. But tonight that changes. The Romantic Tour launches and it starts big, with Bruno Mars playing American football stadiums for the very first time in his career.

It has been close to a decade since Bruno Mars last did a full world tour. His 24K Magic World Tour wrapped in 2017 and became one of the highest-grossing tours of that year. Since then he has played residencies, appeared at events, and of course teamed up with Anderson .Paak as Silk Sonic. But a proper globe-spanning tour? This is new territory again.

Football stadiums are a different beast from arenas. They hold tens of thousands more people and the production has to scale up massively to match. The fact that Bruno Mars is tackling them for the first time says a lot about where his star power sits right now. The Romantic Tour is going to be one of the biggest live music stories of 2026.
keyPoints: The Romantic Tour kicks off tonight on Friday 10 April 2026
This is Bruno Mars's first world tour in nearly ten years
Bruno Mars is playing American football stadiums for the very first time in his career
He spent recent years doing hugely successful residency shows in Las Vegas
His last world tour, the 24K Magic World Tour, was one of the highest-grossing of 2017

articles.title: OpenAI Launches $100 Per Month ChatGPT Pro Tier
articles.summary: OpenAI has announced a new $100 per month Pro subscription for ChatGPT, aimed squarely at coders and power users who need serious capacity. The new Pro tier offers five times more usage of OpenAI's Codex coding tool compared to the existing $20 per month Plus subscription.
articles.content:
OpenAI just made a bold move for its most serious users. The company has launched a new ChatGPT Pro subscription priced at $100 per month, sitting well above the existing ChatGPT Plus plan at $20 per month. The big selling point is access to Codex, OpenAI's coding focused AI tool, at five times the usage limits you get with ChatGPT Plus.

Codex is built for writing, debugging and working through complex coding tasks. OpenAI is pitching the new ChatGPT Pro tier as 'best for longer, high effort Codex sessions', which tells you exactly who they are targeting here. Professional developers, engineers and anyone who uses AI to write serious amounts of code on a daily basis are the audience for this.

The pricing jump from $20 to $100 is steep, but OpenAI is clearly betting there is a big enough market of professionals who will pay for the extra headroom. As AI coding tools become more central to how software gets built, expect this kind of tiered pricing to become the norm across the industry.
keyPoints: OpenAI has launched a new $100 per month ChatGPT Pro subscription
ChatGPT Pro offers five times more Codex usage than the $20 ChatGPT Plus plan
Codex is OpenAI's AI tool designed specifically for coding tasks
The tier is aimed at professional developers who need heavy daily usage
This signals a broader trend of premium AI pricing for power users

articles.title: Noah Kahan Announces The Great Divide World Tour
articles.summary: Noah Kahan has revealed The Great Divide World Tour, hitting the UK, Europe, Australia, and New Zealand. This is the biggest international run yet for the Vermont singer-songwriter and fans have been waiting for this.
articles.content:
Noah Kahan is coming for you. The singer-songwriter just announced The Great Divide World Tour, with dates confirmed across the UK, Europe, Australia, and New Zealand. His words when he broke the news? "We are coming to bring you great sadness and reflection." Very on brand.

Noah Kahan broke through in a big way with his album Stick Season back in 2022, turning small-town Vermont anxiety into massive stadium feelings. Since then he has gone from indie folk favourite to one of the most talked about live acts around. This tour feels like the natural next step.

If you have been sleeping on Noah Kahan live, now is the time to wake up. His shows are the kind where people cry and sing every word at the same time. Grab tickets early because this one is going to move fast.
keyPoints: Noah Kahan has announced The Great Divide World Tour
Tour covers the UK, Europe, Australia, and New Zealand
Noah Kahan teased the tour with the line about bringing 'great sadness and reflection'
He rose to fame with Stick Season in 2022 and has been a live phenomenon since
Tickets are expected to sell out quickly given his recent momentum

articles.title: Molotov Cocktail Thrown at Sam Altman's SF Home
articles.summary: A 20-year-old suspect was arrested after throwing a Molotov cocktail at OpenAI CEO Sam Altman's $27 million San Francisco home. The attack highlights how much hostility is building around AI's most visible figures.
articles.content:
Someone threw a Molotov cocktail at Sam Altman's house in San Francisco on Friday. Police arrested a 20-year-old suspect quickly and charges are pending. OpenAI publicly praised the San Francisco Police Department for how fast they responded.

Sam Altman is the CEO of OpenAI, the company behind ChatGPT. He is probably the most recognisable face of the AI boom right now, which makes him a lightning rod for people who are angry about where AI is taking the world. A $27 million home in San Francisco is already a symbol of tech wealth, and that tension is clearly boiling over for some people.

No one was reported hurt, which is a relief. But this is a stark reminder that the backlash against AI and the people leading it is not just happening online. OpenAI has not said much beyond praising the police response, but expect this story to keep growing.
keyPoints: A 20-year-old suspect was arrested after the attack on Sam Altman's San Francisco home
Charges are pending against the suspect
OpenAI praised the SFPD for their quick response
No injuries were reported
The incident reflects growing real-world anger toward AI leaders

articles.title: Pink Is Hosting the 2026 Tony Awards and She's Ready
articles.summary: Pink has been announced as the host of the 2026 Tony Awards, the biggest night in American theatre. Even though Pink has never performed on Broadway herself, her music has a real home there through Moulin Rouge! The Musical and the jukebox show & Juliet.
articles.content:
Pink is heading to Broadway, just not in the way anyone expected. The pop rock icon has been tapped to host the 2026 Tony Awards, and she is clearly excited about it. Pink said in a statement 'I cannot wait', which honestly feels very on brand for someone who has spent decades doing things her own way.

The Tony Awards are theatre's equivalent of the Oscars, celebrating the best of Broadway each season. Pink has a genuine connection to the world of musical theatre even without stepping on stage herself. Her songs are featured in Moulin Rouge! The Musical, the Broadway adaptation of the beloved Baz Luhrmann film, and & Juliet, the jukebox musical that uses pop hits to retell the story of Juliet after Romeo dies.

This is a smart and fun pick. Pink brings real energy and a massive fanbase to a ceremony that is always looking to reach new audiences. Expect some surprises and probably at least one show stopping moment from a host who does not really do boring.
keyPoints: Pink will host the 2026 Tony Awards
The Tony Awards are Broadway's biggest annual night, like the Oscars for theatre
Pink's music appears in both Moulin Rouge! The Musical and & Juliet on Broadway
Pink has not performed on Broadway herself but has strong ties to the scene
This is a high profile booking designed to pull in new audiences

articles.title: Iranian Creators Behind Viral Lego AI Videos Explain Their Approach
articles.summary: A wave of AI-generated Lego videos made by Iranian creators is going viral, offering a very different take on the story of a US airman rescued after his fighter jet was downed behind Iranian borders. The creators say the secret to their reach is simple: heart.
articles.content:
Donald Trump has framed the recent rescue of a downed American airman as a win, a story of US military might and a successful recovery operation. But on social media, a series of AI-generated Lego videos telling the same story from an Iranian perspective has racked up millions of views. The videos are colourful, playful, and pointed, and they are made by creators based in Iran using AI video tools to animate Lego-style characters.

The creators spoke to The Verge about why they think their videos spread so fast. Their answer was not about algorithms or posting strategies. They said the key ingredient is heart, meaning genuine emotion and a point of view that feels human even when the visuals are machine-made. That is a fascinating idea. AI-generated content often gets criticised for feeling cold or hollow, but these creators are arguing that the intent behind the video matters more than how it is made.

This is a genuinely new kind of political storytelling. People in countries with restricted media access are using accessible AI tools to produce content that travels globally and challenges dominant narratives. Whether you agree with the message or not, the format is worth paying attention to. AI video is becoming a tool for counter-storytelling, and this is one of the clearest examples yet.
articles.wikiSearchQuery: AI generated video political content social media
keyPoints: Iranian creators are making AI-generated Lego videos that retell the US airman rescue story from their own perspective
The videos have gone viral globally and offer a counter-narrative to Donald Trump's framing of events
The creators say emotional authenticity, not technical tricks, is why their videos spread
This shows how AI video tools are giving people in restricted media environments a new way to reach global audiences
The trend is a early signal of AI-generated content being used for political counter-storytelling

articles.title: The Rolling Stones Tease Summer Album via Secret Band Stunt
articles.summary: Mysterious posters for a band called the Cockroaches have appeared around Camden Town in north London, and the Cockroaches are releasing a vinyl-only white label single called "Rough And Twisted" this Saturday. Everything points to this being a Rolling Stones stunt signalling a new summer album.
articles.content:
Here is what we know. Posters for a band called the Cockroaches started showing up around Camden Town in north London this week. Camden is a legendary music neighbourhood, home to venues and record shops that have been part of British rock history for decades. The Cockroaches have a website and they are dropping a vinyl-only white label single called "Rough And Twisted" on Saturday. White label releases are records pressed without full artwork or branding, usually used to build mystery around a release.

Nobody is seriously pretending the Cockroaches are a real new band. Every signal points to the Rolling Stones. The timing, the location, the old-school vinyl stunt, all of it has the fingerprints of a classic rock band building anticipation for something bigger. Stereogum reports this looks like a signal that a full summer album is on the way from Mick Jagger and the band.

The Rolling Stones released their last studio album, Hackney Diamonds, in October 2023. It was their first album of original material in eighteen years and it was warmly received. If "Rough And Twisted" is indeed the first taste of new Rolling Stones music, the summer of 2026 just got a lot more interesting.
keyPoints: Mysterious posters for a band called the Cockroaches appeared in Camden Town in north London
The Cockroaches are releasing a vinyl-only white label single called Rough And Twisted this Saturday
Everything strongly suggests this is a Rolling Stones teaser campaign for a new summer album
The Rolling Stones last released Hackney Diamonds in October 2023, their first original studio album in eighteen years
White label vinyl releases are a classic way to build mystery around new music before an official announcement

articles.title: Wit Studio Apologizes for AI Use in Ascendance of a Bookworm
articles.summary: Wit Studio has confirmed it used generative AI in the opening sequence of Ascendance of a Bookworm Season 4 and has apologized to fans. The studio will redraw the opening, but the incident has sparked a wider debate about AI creeping into professional anime production.
articles.content:
Wit Studio, the Tokyo animation house behind Attack on Titan Seasons 1 through 3 and Spy x Family, is in damage control mode. After fans raised suspicions that parts of the Season 4 opening of Ascendance of a Bookworm looked off, Wit Studio launched an internal investigation. That investigation confirmed it. Generative AI was used in the sequence, and Wit Studio has now issued a formal apology.

Ascendance of a Bookworm is a popular isekai anime, meaning a fantasy story where a character is transported to another world. In this case, a book-obsessed young woman is reborn in a medieval world with no books and decides to make her own. The show has a passionate fanbase who care deeply about its visual craft, so the AI discovery stung.

Wit Studio has promised to redraw the opening from scratch. That is the right call, but the fact it happened at all is the real story. Anime studios are notoriously overworked and underpaid, which makes the temptation to cut corners with AI very real. Fans and industry watchers will now be watching Wit Studio, and every other studio, much more closely.
articles.wikiSearchQuery: Ascendance of a Bookworm anime series
keyPoints: Wit Studio confirmed generative AI was used in the Ascendance of a Bookworm Season 4 opening
The studio has apologized and will redraw the opening sequence entirely
Wit Studio is best known for Attack on Titan and Spy x Family
The incident highlights pressure on anime studios to cut corners under tight schedules
Fans spotted the AI usage before any official admission, showing how alert anime audiences have become

articles.title: TikToker Daniel Larson Pleads Guilty, Faces 5 Years in Prison
articles.summary: Daniel Larson, a TikToker who became notorious online for erratic behaviour and disturbing content, has pleaded guilty in federal court to a charge connected to a series of bomb threats he made online. He now faces up to five years in prison.
articles.content:
Daniel Larson has pleaded guilty in federal court. The charge is tied to a wave of online bomb threats he made, and newly filed court documents confirm the guilty plea. He is now facing up to five years behind bars.

If you are not familiar with Daniel Larson, he became a strange and troubling fixture of TikTok and broader internet culture over the past few years. He built a massive following, partly because people found his unpredictable behaviour compelling to watch. But behind the viral moments, there were repeated incidents involving threats and erratic actions that eventually crossed into serious criminal territory. Bomb threats are not internet drama. They are federal crimes.

This case is a pretty clear line in the sand moment for creator culture. The internet has a habit of platforming people whose behaviour is genuinely dangerous, treating it like entertainment rather than a warning sign. Daniel Larson's guilty plea is a reminder that what happens online has real consequences, and not just for the people making the threats.
keyPoints: Daniel Larson has pleaded guilty in federal court to a charge linked to online bomb threats
He faces up to five years in prison
Larson became widely known through TikTok before his behaviour escalated into criminal activity
The guilty plea was confirmed through newly filed court documents
The case is a stark reminder that online threats carry serious real-world legal consequences

articles.title: Fifty Fifty Cover Pink Floyd and Prove K-Pop Has No Limits
articles.summary: K-pop group Fifty Fifty, best known for their viral 2023 hit Cupid, have covered Pink Floyd and are telling the world their music transcends language. It is a bold move and it is working.
articles.content:
Fifty Fifty are not your typical K-pop act and they want you to know it. The South Korean group first exploded onto the global scene in 2023 with Cupid, a track that became one of those TikTok songs you heard absolutely everywhere. Now they have covered a Pink Floyd classic and the reaction has been wild.

Cupid was a massive deal. It became one of the most streamed K-pop songs ever by a rookie group and introduced Fifty Fifty to millions of people who had never paid much attention to K-pop before. Going from that sugary earworm to a Pink Floyd cover is a statement move. Fifty Fifty are clearly not interested in staying in one lane.

The group's quote says it all: "Our music transcends language." That is not just confidence, it is a mission statement. K-pop has been crossing borders for years but Fifty Fifty reaching into classic rock territory feels like a new kind of crossover. Keep an eye on these four.
keyPoints: Fifty Fifty have covered a Pink Floyd classic, marking a major genre leap
Fifty Fifty first became famous with Cupid in 2023, a massive global TikTok hit
The group says their music transcends language, signalling serious crossover ambitions
Going from a viral K-pop earworm to Pink Floyd is a bold and unusual artistic move
Fifty Fifty are positioning themselves as one of the most versatile acts in K-pop right now

articles.title: YouTube Premium Price Rise Triggers Mass Cancellations
articles.summary: YouTube has announced a price increase for YouTube Premium Individual subscribers, and a wave of users have already cancelled their subscriptions in response. It matters because YouTube Premium is one of the most widely used paid streaming add-ons, and pushback this loud could force YouTube to reconsider its strategy.
articles.content:
YouTube just told its YouTube Premium Individual subscribers that they will be paying more, and a lot of people are not happy about it. Social media has been flooded with people saying they have already cancelled or are planning to the moment the new price kicks in. The backlash has been fast and pretty loud.

YouTube Premium gives subscribers an ad-free experience, background play on mobile, and access to YouTube Music. The Individual plan is the most popular tier, so a price rise there hits the biggest chunk of the paying user base. YouTube has raised prices before, including a notable increase in 2023, but each time it happens the reaction gets a little more intense as subscribers feel the overall cost of their streaming subscriptions piling up.

The real tension here is that YouTube is essentially unavoidable for a huge portion of internet users. But unavoidable does not mean people will pay anything to use it comfortably. If enough YouTube Premium subscribers cancel and stick with free, ad-supported YouTube instead, that could actually hurt YouTube's premium revenue more than the price rise gains. YouTube will be watching the cancellation numbers very closely.
keyPoints: YouTube has announced a price increase for YouTube Premium Individual subscribers
Many subscribers have already cancelled their YouTube Premium plans in response
YouTube Premium includes ad-free viewing, background play, and YouTube Music
This is not the first YouTube Premium price rise but the backlash is significant
Mass cancellations could undercut any revenue gains YouTube hoped to make

articles.title: Bluesky COO Rose Wang on Hitting 40 Million Users
articles.summary: Bluesky COO Rose Wang has spoken about how Bluesky grew to 40 million users and why the platform's approach could define the future of social media. Bluesky is no longer just a Twitter alternative, it is starting to look like something genuinely new.
articles.content:
Bluesky has hit 40 million users and COO Rose Wang is talking about how they got there. The short version is that Bluesky built trust by doing things differently. No algorithmic chaos shoved in your face, no billionaire tweeting at 2am about the platform he owns. Just a cleaner, more user-controlled experience that people actually wanted.

Bluesky launched as a project backed by Twitter co-founder Jack Dorsey, with the goal of building a decentralised social network where users had more control. It spent a long time in invite-only mode before opening up, and a big wave of users arrived after Elon Musk's changes at X made a lot of people look for the exit.

Rose Wang's point is that Bluesky earned its reputation rather than buying it. That is rare in the social media world. With 40 million users and a clear philosophy, Bluesky is starting to feel less like a protest platform and more like a genuine contender.
keyPoints: Bluesky has reached 40 million users according to COO Rose Wang
Rose Wang says Bluesky's growth came from earning trust, not marketing spend
Bluesky was originally backed by Twitter co-founder Jack Dorsey as a decentralised social project
Many users joined Bluesky after Elon Musk's changes at X drove people to look elsewhere
Bluesky's user-controlled approach is being held up as a possible model for social media's future

articles.title: Vans Slip-On Gets a Quilted Chanel Classic Flap Makeover
articles.summary: Vans has released a quilted version of its iconic Classic Slip-On that takes direct design inspiration from the Chanel Classic Flap Bag, one of the most recognisable luxury handbags ever made. It is a wild crossover between skate culture and high fashion that somehow works completely.
articles.content:
Nobody had 'Chanel meets Vans' on their fashion bingo card for 2026, but here we are. The Vans Classic Slip-On has been reimagined with quilted detailing that is a clear nod to the Chanel Classic Flap Bag, the diamond quilted leather handbag that has been a symbol of luxury fashion since Coco Chanel introduced it in the 1950s. The Chanel Classic Flap Bag is one of those rare objects that has influenced basically every accessories designer working in the decades since.

The Vans Classic Slip-On is itself a design icon of a very different world. Born out of California skate culture in the 1970s, the slip-on silhouette has been on feet at skate parks, music festivals and art school campuses for generations. Putting Chanel-coded quilting onto that canvas is a genuinely unexpected move. It is the kind of mash-up that sounds wrong in theory and looks completely right in practice.

This is fashion doing what fashion does best. Taking two things that live in completely different worlds and making you wish they had always existed together. The quilted Vans Slip-On is going to be everywhere by summer.
keyPoints: Vans has released a quilted Classic Slip-On inspired by the Chanel Classic Flap Bag
The Chanel Classic Flap Bag is one of the most influential luxury accessories ever made
The Vans Classic Slip-On has been a skate and street culture staple since the 1970s
The quilted design is a striking high-low fashion crossover moment
Expect this collaboration to trend hard heading into summer

articles.title: Gorillaz and Sparks Bring 'Happy Dictator' to Kimmel
articles.summary: Gorillaz joined forces with Sparks to perform 'Happy Dictator' on Jimmy Kimmel Live, marking a rare late-night U.S. TV appearance for the virtual band. It is one of the most interesting stage pairings you will see on a late-night show this year.
articles.content:
Gorillaz took a break from their touring schedule to show up on Jimmy Kimmel Live and perform 'Happy Dictator' alongside Sparks. Gorillaz, the cartoon band created by Damon Albarn and Jamie Hewlett, do not do a lot of traditional U.S. TV appearances, so this was genuinely worth stopping and watching. Sparks are the veteran art-pop duo made up of brothers Ron and Russell Mael, who have been making eccentric music since the early 1970s.

The collaboration between Gorillaz and Sparks feels very natural once you see it. Both acts have always operated in their own weird, brilliant lane, far outside mainstream pop conventions. Putting them together for 'Happy Dictator' on Kimmel is the kind of booking that makes late-night TV feel exciting again.

Gorillaz are currently on tour, so catching a live TV performance from them is a treat for fans who are not near a tour date. If 'Happy Dictator' is new to you, the Kimmel performance is a great place to start. Go watch it.
keyPoints: Gorillaz performed 'Happy Dictator' on Jimmy Kimmel Live
Sparks joined Gorillaz for the performance
This was a rare late-night U.S. TV appearance for Gorillaz
Gorillaz are currently in the middle of a touring run
Sparks are a pioneering art-pop duo who have been active since the early 1970s

articles.title: 100+ Self-Driving Cars Strand Passengers in Wuhan Outage
articles.summary: More than 100 self-driving taxis in Wuhan, China suddenly stopped working at the same time, leaving passengers stuck in heavy traffic. It is a very public reminder that autonomous vehicles still have a long way to go before they can be fully trusted.
articles.content:
Picture being stuck in a driverless taxi that just... stops. That is exactly what happened in Wuhan, China on Friday. Over 100 self-driving cars went offline all at once during what appears to have been a sudden system outage. Passengers were left stranded in heavy traffic with no driver to take the wheel and no clear explanation coming fast enough.

Wuhan has been one of the most ambitious testing grounds for autonomous vehicle technology in China. The city has leaned heavily into driverless taxis as a vision of the future of urban transport. An outage at this scale is not just an inconvenience. It is a very visible crack in that vision, and it happened in the worst possible conditions: a busy city, heavy traffic, and a lot of frustrated people going nowhere.

The companies involved have not yet confirmed what caused the simultaneous shutdown. But the images of over 100 idle autonomous vehicles clogging up Wuhan's streets are already spreading fast online. For anyone watching the self-driving car industry, this is the kind of moment that sets public trust back significantly.
keyPoints: Over 100 self-driving cars in Wuhan, China went offline at the same time
Passengers were left stranded in heavy traffic with no way to take control
Wuhan is one of China's biggest test cities for autonomous vehicle technology
The cause of the simultaneous outage has not been confirmed
The incident is a major blow to public confidence in driverless car technology

articles.title: Stefano Gabbana Steps Down as Dolce and Gabbana Chairman
articles.summary: Stefano Gabbana has stepped down as Chairman of Dolce and Gabbana, marking the end of an era for one of fashion's most recognisable names. The same week also saw Fear of God part ways with its CEO and Dior announce a major runway show in Los Angeles.
articles.content:
Stefano Gabbana co-founded Dolce and Gabbana with Domenico Dolce back in 1985 in Milan. The brand became one of the most iconic in luxury fashion, famous for its bold Italian aesthetic, ornate prints, and a showmanship that few could match. Gabbana stepping away from the Chairman role is a seismic shift for a house that has always been built around its founders' personalities.

It is a busy week for fashion reshuffles. Fear of God, the luxury American label founded by Jerry Lorenzo, has also said goodbye to its CEO. Meanwhile Dior has announced it will stage a runway show in Los Angeles, a move that signals how seriously European luxury houses are courting American cultural power. Saks Global is also reportedly close to exiting bankruptcy after securing new funding, which would be significant news for US department store retail.

And Hermès is expanding its physical store footprint, doubling down on in-person retail at a time when many brands are retreating online. All of this together paints a picture of a fashion industry in real transition right now. Leadership is changing, geography is shifting, and the old rules are being rewritten.
keyPoints: Stefano Gabbana has stepped down as Chairman of Dolce and Gabbana
Fear of God has also parted ways with its CEO in the same week
Dior has announced a runway show in Los Angeles
Saks Global is close to exiting bankruptcy with new funding secured
Hermès is expanding its physical retail presence rather than going fully digital

articles.title: Moonmax Is Making a Captain James Hook AI Film
articles.summary: AI studio Moonmax has announced a Captain James Hook feature film as part of a new push into mainstream movie production. This is a big moment because it signals AI studios moving beyond short-form content and into full Hollywood territory.
articles.content:
Moonmax, an AI studio best known for its AI courses and content tools, just announced something nobody saw coming. The company is developing a feature film based on Captain James Hook, the iconic villain from J. M. Barrie's Peter Pan stories. This is part of a wider slate of projects that show Moonmax is done playing small.

Captain James Hook is one of the most recognisable characters in fiction, a swaggering, revenge-obsessed pirate who has shown up in everything from stage plays to Steven Spielberg's Hook in 1991. The fact that Moonmax is planting its flag on this character tells you something. They are not picking an obscure IP. They are going straight for the mainstream.

The big question is what AI-assisted actually means here. Does Moonmax use AI for the script, the visuals, the voice work, or all of it? That detail will matter a lot to audiences and to Hollywood. Either way, this project puts Moonmax on the map as a studio to watch.
keyPoints: Moonmax is an AI studio now moving into full feature film production
The Captain James Hook project is based on J. M. Barrie's classic pirate villain
This is part of a broader Moonmax slate targeting mainstream audiences
The project raises fresh questions about how far AI involvement goes in filmmaking
Hollywood will be watching this closely as a test case for AI-made features
articles.wikiSearchQuery: Captain James Hook J. M. Barrie Peter Pan

articles.title: Snap and Qualcomm Team Up to Revive AI Glasses
articles.summary: Snap has partnered with Qualcomm to push its augmented reality glasses project forward after years of slow progress. This deal signals that Snap is serious about finally getting its AR glasses into the real world.
articles.content:
Snap has been teasing augmented reality glasses for what feels like forever. Now Snap has announced a partnership with Qualcomm that could actually make it happen. Qualcomm makes the chips that power a huge number of smart devices, so having them on board is a real sign that Snap is moving from concept to product.

Snap has been in a long and painful holding pattern on its AR glasses ambitions. The company has shown off prototypes and talked a big game about augmented reality for years, but nothing has shipped to consumers at scale. This Qualcomm partnership is the most concrete step forward Snap has taken in a while.

The race for AR glasses is heating up. Meta has its Ray-Ban smart glasses already out in the world, and Apple has Vision Pro in the mix. Snap getting Qualcomm involved suggests Snap wants a real seat at the table, not just a slide deck full of promises.
keyPoints: Snap has partnered with Qualcomm to develop its next augmented reality glasses
Snap has been working on AR glasses for years without releasing a consumer product
Qualcomm's chip expertise gives the project much more credibility
Snap is entering a market where Meta and Apple already have products out
This is the most significant movement on Snap's AR glasses in a long time
articles.wikiSearchQuery: Snap Inc augmented reality glasses Spectacles

articles.title: Metal Gear Solid Movie Finally Moving Forward With New Directors
articles.summary: A new Metal Gear Solid film is in development, helmed by the directors of Final Destination: Bloodlines. This is the best news for Metal Gear fans in years after the Oscar Isaac version quietly fell apart.
articles.content:
A Metal Gear Solid movie is back on the table. The directors of Final Destination: Bloodlines are now attached to bring Hideo Kojima's iconic stealth game franchise to the big screen. No cast has been announced yet, but the project finally has real momentum behind it.

For context, a previous Metal Gear Solid adaptation had been in the works for years with Oscar Isaac set to play the lead role of Snake. That version never made it past the announcement stage, leaving fans with nothing but hype and disappointment. This new attempt comes with directors who have fresh Hollywood heat thanks to Final Destination: Bloodlines.

Metal Gear Solid is one of gaming's most cinematic franchises, full of twisty spy plots, massive boss fights, and long dramatic cutscenes. If any video game deserves a proper film treatment, it is this one. The question now is whether the new team can actually get it over the finish line.
keyPoints: A new Metal Gear Solid film is officially in development
The directors of Final Destination: Bloodlines are attached to the project
A previous version with Oscar Isaac as Snake never got made
No cast has been announced yet
Metal Gear Solid is known for its cinematic storytelling, making it a strong film candidate

articles.title: Tom DeLonge Once Showed Trent Reznor a Dead Alien Photo
articles.summary: Ilan Rubin revealed in a recent podcast interview that Tom DeLonge pulled out a photo of what he claimed was a dead alien at Rubin's own wedding, and showed it to Trent Reznor. Rubin described DeLonge's enthusiasm for extraterrestrial topics as 'juvenile excitement', and honestly that makes this story even better.
articles.content:
Wedding receptions usually involve toasts and dancing. Ilan Rubin's apparently also involved Tom DeLonge showing Trent Reznor a photograph of a supposed dead alien. Rubin, who plays drums in Nine Inch Nails alongside Reznor and is also a member of Angels and Airwaves with DeLonge, shared the story on a recent podcast and could not help but laugh about it.

Rubin described DeLonge's approach as 'juvenile excitement', which is honestly the most affectionate way anyone has ever summed up the blink-182 co-founder's very public obsession with UFOs and extraterrestrial life. DeLonge has spent years pursuing this interest seriously, co-founding To The Stars Academy of Arts and Science, an organization dedicated to researching UFO phenomena. Trent Reznor, known for being one of the more intense and serious figures in rock music, receiving an alien photo at a wedding reception is a combination nobody asked for but everyone needed.

This story has absolutely no cultural stakes but it is a perfect little window into the weird, warm world of rock musicians hanging out together. DeLonge is going to be DeLonge wherever he is. Even at a wedding. Even next to Trent Reznor.
keyPoints: Tom DeLonge showed Trent Reznor a photo of a supposed dead alien at Ilan Rubin's wedding
Ilan Rubin plays in both Nine Inch Nails with Reznor and Angels and Airwaves with DeLonge
Rubin described DeLonge's extraterrestrial enthusiasm as 'juvenile excitement'
DeLonge co-founded To The Stars Academy to research UFO phenomena
The story came out in a recent podcast interview with Rubin
articles.wikiSearchQuery: Tom DeLonge UFO To The Stars Academy
